title: Decision Mode Governance Rules
description: JSON Schema for governance rules applied to macp.mode.decision.v1 sessions. See RFC-MACP-0012 Section 4.1.

algorithm.description: Voting algorithm. 'none' means no voting constraint is enforced.

threshold.description: Fraction of cast votes required for approval. Used by 'supermajority' and 'weighted' algorithms.

quorum.description: Minimum participation required before votes are counted.

value.description: Minimum count or percentage (0-1) of participants that must vote.

weights.description: Per-participant vote weights. Keys are participant IDs. Used only with 'weighted' algorithm.

objection_handling.description: How blocking objections affect commitment eligibility.

critical_severity_vetoes.description: If true, objections with severity 'critical' prevent commitment.

veto_threshold.description: Number of blocking objections required to veto commitment.

critical_objection_action.description: Action when a critical objection would block commitment: deny (reject), finalize_decline (resolve as a negative outcome), or hold (leave the session open).

evaluation.description: Constraints on evaluations before voting or commitment.

minimum_confidence.description: Minimum confidence level for evaluations to be considered valid.

required_before_voting.description: If true, at least one evaluation must exist before votes are counted.

commitment.description: Who can commit and under what conditions.

authority.description: Who is authorized to emit a Commitment.

designated_roles.description: Participant IDs or roles authorized to commit. Used only with 'designated_role' authority.

require_vote_quorum.description: If true, commitment is only allowed after vote quorum is met.

allow_decline_over_approval.description: If true, a negative commitment (outcome_positive=false) is allowed even when the vote passed.
